page: Amazon Web Services g4dn.metal is a bare-metal, GPU-accelerated server designed for graphics-intensive and parallel computing workloads. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors operating at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

The hardware configuration includes eight NVIDIA T4 GPUs based on the Turing architecture, delivering a combined 128 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 1800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This high-density setup is optimized for both local storage access and high-throughput network operations.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores placing in the top 25 percent. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with read-only large block speeds reaching 219.14 GB/sec and write speeds at 95.27 GB/sec. However, single-core CPU performance is moderate, with Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core scores falling in the middle 50 percent range, indicating a tradeoff where the system excels in parallelized tasks rather than single-threaded execution.

Given its dense GPU array, high vCPU count, and strong database and Redis performance of 77.26 million operations per second, this server is suited for multi-stream video transcoding, machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and high-throughput database operations. The bare-metal architecture provides direct hardware access, maximizing resource density and cost efficiency for workloads that can fully utilize parallel GPU and CPU execution.
description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.metal is a bare-metal, GPU-accelerated server designed for parallel computing and graphics-intensive workloads. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The server features eight NVIDIA T4 GPUs with 128 GB of total VRAM, 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, though single-core performance remains average. This hardware profile makes the server highly efficient for multi-stream video transcoding, machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and high-throughput database operations.

tagline: A bare-metal server featuring eight GPUs, high-density multi-core processing, and high-speed local NVMe storage for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM
Eight NVIDIA T4 GPUs providing 128 GB total VRAM
1800 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps network bandwidth
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for machine learning inference, video transcoding, and databases
